claim_text: Reduced scope-1 and scope-2 greenhouse gas emissions by 20% since 2019
verification_note: Baseline year changed in 2021 restatement — before/after comparison is not possible without restated prior data

claim_text: Invested $5bn in low-carbon energy in 2023
verification_note: Total capex was $16.3bn — internal breakdowns show 74% directed to oil and gas extraction and production

claim_text: Zero routine flaring commitment by 2030
verification_note: No interim milestones disclosed; current flaring volumes not consistently reported year-on-year

claim_text: 50% reduction in methane intensity by 2023 versus 2016 baseline
verification_note: CDP data and independent third-party verification confirm approximately 47% reduction as of 2023 — within rounding of claim

claim_text: Net zero across operations and production by 2050 or sooner
verification_note: No enforceable interim milestones; executive remuneration not linked to 2050 target; subject of FCA inquiry

claim_text: Reduced carbon intensity of energy generation by 87% since 2006
verification_note: Annual reports and EY third-party audit confirm 87% reduction — consistent across all reporting periods

claim_text: 87% of heat and power output from renewable energy sources in 2023
verification_note: Independently audited; fully consistent with asset portfolio composition (offshore wind / bioenergy)

claim_text: Science-based targets validated by SBTi for net zero by 2040
verification_note: SBTi approval on public registry — consistent with 1.5°C pathway; strongest validation tier

claim_text: Zero biodiversity net loss from all new energy projects
verification_note: Methodology for biodiversity net gain accounting is not independently verified; no third-party auditor named

claim_text: Achieved net-zero emissions from own operations in 2023
verification_note: Achieved entirely through purchased offsets — absolute Scope 1+2 emissions remained flat; offset quality contested

claim_text: Reduce carbon intensity of energy products by 30% by 2035 vs 2016
verification_note: Intensity (not absolute) metric — oil and gas production increases could raise absolute emissions while meeting this target

claim_text: Working to reduce operated methane intensity across all assets
verification_note: No specific targets or baselines provided — progress is unquantifiable; no third-party verification

claim_text: Investing in lower-emission technology research and development
verification_note: R&D spend on low-emission technology not separately disclosed from conventional R&D

claim_text: Reduced methane emissions by 50% by 2025 versus 2020 baseline
verification_note: Third-party monitoring data shows 48% reduction by 2023 — on track; broadly consistent with claim

claim_text: Carbon neutrality of worldwide operations by 2050
verification_note: Relies heavily on carbon offsets; forest carbon projects in portfolio have faced scrutiny from Verra

claim_text: 100% renewable electricity across all global manufacturing operations since 2020
verification_note: RE100 membership and annual disclosures consistent — third-party verified by Bureau Veritas

claim_text: Halved absolute carbon footprint across full value chain by 2030 target
verification_note: Scope 3 represents 99% of footprint; progress on these not independently verified; consumer use phase excluded

claim_text: On track to achieve net zero across full value chain by 2050
verification_note: CDP gives C rating; own disclosures show Scope 3 emissions increased 2021–2023; inconsistent with net zero trajectory

claim_text: Achieved 20% absolute GHG emissions reduction target by 2025
verification_note: Own 2023 disclosures show absolute Scope 1+2 emissions higher than 2018 baseline — target missed and quietly restated

claim_text: 100% of cocoa sustainably sourced through Rainforest Alliance certification
verification_note: Rainforest Alliance methodology is contested — does not verify child labour absence; supply chain mapping incomplete

claim_text: Halved use of virgin plastics per tonne of product since 2018
verification_note: Absolute virgin plastic use increased from 1.5Mt to 1.7Mt (2018–2023); intensity metric obscures absolute growth

claim_text: Net-zero carbon across all operations and supply chain by 2040
verification_note: AWS data centre energy growth is outpacing renewable procurement; Scope 3 supply chain emissions not addressed in targets

claim_text: 50% of shipments delivered with net-zero carbon in 2023
verification_note: Methodology disputed by Carbon Brief; scope of 'net-zero shipment' not independently verified; relies on in-house offsets
